content.title: Mega Evolution
content.body: Mega Evolutions, a feature not seen since Pokemon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, make its reapperance in Pokemon Champions. This is the only available battle mechanic at the time of the game's launch, in Regulation Set M-A.

content.title: Terastalization
content.body: Terastallization is expected to be added in Pokemon Champions. The trailer shows the other trainer's Dondozo terastallizing just after the Charizard mega evolves.

content.title: Z-Moves
content.body: The Z-Move symbol is featured on the artwork for the Omni Ring, a newly introduced device in Pokemon Champions. This heavily suggests that Z-Moves will be in the game, and it is likely that the Omni Ring will be the one device for all battle mechanics.

content.title: Dynamax
content.body: Dynamax is rumored to be usable in Pokemon Champions, as the bottom part of Dynamax's symbol can be seen on the Omni Ring image.

content.title: Stat Alignment List and Chart
content.body: Stats Increased by Stat Alignments Atk Def SpAtk SpDef Speed None

content.title: Stat Alignments That Increase Attack
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Lonely Attack Defense Adamant Attack SpAtk Naughty Attack SpDef Brave Attack Speed

content.title: Stat Alignments That Increase Defense
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Bold Defense Attack Impish Defense SpAtk Lax Defense SpDef Relaxed Defense Speed

content.title: Stat Alignments That Increase Special Attack
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Modest SpAtk Attack Mild SpAtk Defense Rash SpAtk SpDef Quiet SpAtk Speed

content.title: Stat Alignments That Increase Special Defense
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Calm SpDef Attack Gentle SpDef Defense Careful SpDef SpAtk Sassy SpDef Speed

content.title: Stat Alignments That Increase Speed
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Timid Speed Attack Hasty Speed Defense Jolly Speed SpAtk Naive Speed SpDef

content.title: Stat Alignments That Does Not Increase Stats
content.body: Nature Increased Stat Decreased Stat Serious - -

content.title: Train Your Pokemon and Spend VP Points
content.body: How to Change Stat Alignment 1 Select the Train option from the Main Menu. 2 Choose the Pokemon Training option from the Train Menu. 3 Pick the Pokemon you want to train and select the Stat Alignment tab. 4 From there, choose which Stat Alignment you want your Pokemon to have in the game.

content.title: Training System for Natures
content.body: Stat Alignments are functionally the same as Natures from previous Pokemon games. They still have the same function of increasing and decreasing a certain stat, depending on the Pokemon's nature.

content.title: Requires VP to Train Stat Points
content.body: The VP Cost for assigning Stat Alignment is set at 500 VP , which is more expensive than other training, which still makes it beneficial to catch a Pokemon with the desired Stat Alignment from the start.

timers.page_title: All Battle Timers and Time Limits | Pokemon Champions｜Game8

content.title: Three Different Battle Timers
content.body: Total Time Your Time Move Time (Per Turn)

content.title: The Match Ends in a Draw When the Timer Runs Out
content.body: A Timer Draw occurs during a Ranked or Casual battle where neither player is able to completely take out the enemy team's roster. This is different from previous Pokemon games where the winner is determined by who has more Pokemon alive, or who has more HP left in 1v1 scenarios.

content.title: What is Priority?
content.body: Priority is a property of moves that determines the order that they happen during battle.

content.title: Abilities with Increased Priority
content.body: Ability Details Gale Wings Effect : Increases the priority of the Pokémon's Flying-type moves by 1 stage while its HP is full. Learned by : Prankster Effect : Increases the priority of the Pokémon's status moves by 1 stage. Learned by :

content.title: Abilities Blocking Priority
content.body: Ability Details Armor Tail Effect : Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies. Learned by : Queenly Majesty Effect : Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies. Learned by :

content.title: Moves with Increased Priority
content.body: Increased Priority moves have been an instrumental part of competitive Pokemon as they allow you to gain an advantage in tempo over your opponent. Being able to attack first can put you at an advantage, especially in Doubles.

content.title: Moves with Decreased Priority
content.body: Although it's not ideal to move last, some moves make it worthwhile. Trick Room, the lowest priority move in the game, is a vital strategy in Ranked Battles.

regulation_mb.page_title: Regulation M-B: Complete Roster and Schedule | Pokemon Champions｜Game8

content.title: Same Format as Regulation M-A!
content.body: Regulation M-B will use the same Pokemon and format as Regulation M-A . Additionally, a total of 22 New Pokemon and 16 new Mega Evolutions are available and should shake up the meta with their introduction.

content.title: New Items Introduced!
content.body: New Items has also been added in this Regulation! Notable Items include the Life Orb, Wide Lens, and Light Clay.

content.title: Regulation M-B Runs from June 17 until September 02, 2026
content.body: Start Date June 17, 2026 (Thu) at 02:00 UTC End Date September 02, 2026 (Thu) at 05:59 UTC Available Pokemon ・ Regulation M-B Roster Restricted Pokemon ・None

content.title: Seasons Currently Last 20 Days
content.body: Unlike the previous Ranked Battle Seasons, Season M-3 will last for only 20 days instead of 35 days. It is likely that there will be a third Ranked Battle Season for Regulation M-B instead of the initially announced two.

content.title: All New Pokemon Available in Regulation M-B
content.body: Most of the new Pokemon in this format are the Mega Evolutions that were not available in the previous format, like the Hoenn Starters, Mawile, Metagross, and most of the remaining Z-A Megas.

content.title: All Pokemon in Regulation M-B Roster
content.body: All Pokemon that can currently be transferred into Champions via Pokemon HOME are available to use for Ranked Battle Regulation M-B. Some forms of Vivillon and Furfrou , along with Eternal Flower Floette , are still exclusive via Pokemon HOME transfer and cannot be recruited at the Ranch .

content.title: Details of Legal Teams & Pokemon
content.body: A player's team cannot contain two Pokemon with the same Pokédex number. Your Team can have more than Mega Stone, but you cannot Mega Evolve more than one Pokémon per battle. Two Pokémon cannot hold the same item.

content.title: Regulation M-B Ruleset
content.body: Rankings for Single and Doubles are separate. You cannot go down a rank once your tier has been raised. No matter how many times you lose, your Tier will stay the same. An Internet connection is required in playing Ranked Battles. You will need an Nintendo Switch Online Subscription to participate in all online activities in Pokemon including Ranked Battles.

content.title: How to Recruit Pokemon
content.body: Recruit Every 22 Hours for Free Choose Either Trial or Permanent Recruitment Recruit Pokemon from HOME

content.title: Recruit 1 Pokemon Every 22 Hours For Free
content.body: You can rent or recruit Pokemon in the Roster Ranch. You have one free lineup every 22 hours , which is a set of 10 random Pokemon with preset moves and stats already distributed. You can choose only one Pokemon from each lineup to recruit.

content.title: Choose Either Trial or Permanent Recruitment
content.body: Once you select a Pokemon from the lineup, you will be asked how you would like to recruit that Pokemon. You can choose Trial Recruitment , which allows you to keep the Pokemon for only 7 days , or Permanent Recruitment , which allows you to keep the Pokemon permanently.

content.title: Recruit Pokemon from HOME
content.body: Pokemon that were sent to visit from Pokemon HOME can be collected at the Ranch. Pokemon from HOME function the same as permanently recruited Pokemon, so they can be trained.

content.title: All Ranch Animations
content.body: Shiny Rare Poke Ball Title or Rare Form Regular

content.title: Shiny Animation
content.body: Probably the rarest animation to encounter is the Shiny animation. This animation shows stars shooting out from the silhouette and is accompanied by the Shiny sound effect.

content.title: Rare Poke Ball Rainbow Animation
content.body: If you see a rainbow with some soft circles and sparkles appear when the silhouette pops out, this indicates that at least one Pokemon in the lineup is inside a rare Poke Ball such as Apriballs.

content.title: Titled Pokemon Animation
content.body: The animation that shows fast action lines when focusing on the bush the last Pokemon pops out of indicates that a Pokemon in the lineup has a title or a Pokemon like Vivillon or Furfrou in the lineup has a rare form .

content.title: Regular Animation
content.body: The regular animation has no special effect applied to the Pokemon silhouette. This indicates that the lineup has no special Pokemon.

content.title: Recruitment Items
content.body: Item Description Quick Coupon A coupon that reduces the time you must wait to recruit a Pokemon by one hour. Affinity Ticket A ticket that makes you more likely to meet Pokemon of a specific type in the next lineup. Teammate Ticket A ticket that can be used to permanently recruit a Pokemon.
